mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: The signal predicts negative returns in >60% of expansion periods, or the correlation coefficient between INDPRO growth and 20d returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index annual growth rate surpasses 7.0%, aggressive central bank tightening expectations dominate market pricing, forcing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >58% of periods where CPIAUCSL YoY > 7.0%, or the threshold effect disappears in a quantile regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports consumer confidence and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal generates negative returns in >62% of observations, or the interaction effect is not statistically distinguishable from zero at the 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly measures aggregate economic output and corporate profit potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >65% of periods following positive GDPC1 growth, or the predictive IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production growth outpaces retail sales growth indicates building inventory buffers and supply-side resilience, which reduces cost-push inflation fears and triggers positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal predicts negative returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses significance in a panel regression.
